prompt: We are a federal government agency in Australia running a cloud-hosted case management system that stores OFFICIAL: Sensitive information. We need to understand: which ISM control applicability markings apply to our system, what the key controls we must implement are, and what the authorisation pathway looks like before we can go live.
assertions: States that an OFFICIAL: Sensitive system must implement both NC and OS applicability-marked controls (stacking rule)
Mentions the System Security Plan (SSP) as the primary authorisation artefact
Describes the six-step ISM risk management cycle: define, select, implement, assess, authorise, monitor
Recommends IRAP assessment for OS systems and states it is mandatory for PROTECTED and above
Covers key control domains including system hardening (Ch. 13), patch management (Ch. 14), system monitoring/logging (Ch. 15), and personnel security (Ch. 6)
